rationale: Sargon of Akkad conquered the Sumerian city-states. Same land, new Semitic (Akkadian) ethnicity.
Sumerian revival after Akkadian collapse. Same land, Sumerian ethnicity restored.
Amorite (West Semitic) invasions ended Ur III. Same territory, new Babylonian ethnicity.
Assyrian power rose to dominate the same Mesopotamian heartland. East Semitic, but Assyrian branch.
Chaldean-led revolt destroyed Nineveh. Same territories, Chaldean/Babylonian ethnic revival.
Continuity of Egyptian pharaonic civilization on the same Nile territory.
Assyrian conquest of Egypt under Esarhaddon. Same land, Assyrian ethnicity imposed.
Both Northwest Semitic peoples in the same Levantine territory, contemporaries.
The kingdom split. Judah is the direct southern successor. Same ethnicity, reduced territory.
Babylonian conquest and exile (586 BC). Same land, Chaldean/Babylonian ethnicity.
